docs.title: asdf-vm base images
docs.text:
Works with asdf >=0.16.0

This repository contains base Docker images tailored for the versatile version manager tool, asdf-vm. These base images are designed with a flexible ONBUILD feature, enabling the creation of specialized images pre-equipped with predefined asdf plugins.

docs.title: Base Image Structure
docs.text: The base image Dockerfile provided here serves as a fundamental building block. It includes essential configurations to set up asdf-vm along with necessary tools like Git, curl, and bash within an Linux environment. An ONBUILD directive is employed to facilitate the automatic installation of specified asdf plugins and versions when building new images based on this template.

docs.title: Utilising ToolSet Images
docs.text:
Examples of other Dockerfiles demonstrate how these base images can be utilized to construct specialized images for different tools or software stacks. By leveraging the base image and utilizing a docker-compose file, users can easily set up and install asdf plugins as per their requirements.

To make this your own repository, you can fork this repo and update the following environment variables to your liking.

# labels
NAME="asdf-vm"
MAINTAINER="jrosco"
DESCRIPTION="asdf version manager toolset"
URL="https://github.com/jrosco/docker-container-asdf"
REPOSITORY=$URL

# docker registries
ASDF_REGISTRY_BASE="ghcr.io/jrosco/$NAME"
ASDF_REGISTRY_TOOLSET="$ASDF_REGISTRY_BASE-toolsets"

An example is showcased using Helm packages within an Alpine Linux environment and other examples found in the toolset-docker-images directory.

docker-compose.yml:

services:
  alpine:
        image: ghcr.io/jrosco/asdf-vm-toolsets/helm:alpine
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
      args:
        asdf_image: "ghcr.io/jrosco/asdf-vm:alpine"
        packages: "helm helmfile helm-diff"
      platforms:
        - "linux/amd64"

Example Dockerfile and build:

FROM ghcr.io/jrosco/asdf-vm:alpine

docs.title: Build the image
docs.text:
docker build \
    --build-arg \
    packages="awscli:latest:set helmfile:latest:set helm:latest:set kustomize:latest:set" \
    -t helm-toolset:alpine .

docs.title: Get the installed package versions and details
docs.text:
docker run -it helm-toolset:alpine asdf info
docker run -it helm-toolset:alpine asdf current

docs.title: Docker Build Arguments --build-arg
docs.text:
Each Dockerfile utilizes the following build arguments:

  • asdf_image: The base image to use for the build. This should be one of the base images provided in the base-images directory.
  • packages: A list of asdf plugins to install. These should be space-separated and match the names of the plugins in the asdf-community GitHub repository

packages support the following formats

Format Description helm Install the plugins repo details only (no versions downloaded) helm:3.10.3 Install plugin (if not present) and download helm version 3.10.3 helm:3.10.3:set Install plugin (if not present) and download helm version 3.10.3 and set this as the default version in .tool-versions helm:latest Install plugin (if not present) and download latest version of helm

The asdf_image and packages arguments are optional. If not provided, the build will default to installing the latest version of asdf and no plugins.

docs.title: Docker Image Tagging Strategy
docs.text:
When building toolset images, use a consistent tagging strategy for easy identification and version management. The docker-compose.yaml files in the toolset-docker-images directory demonstrate a multi-tag approach using variables from .env:

tags:
  - "${ASDF_REGISTRY_TOOLSET}/toolset:latest"                                      # Generic latest
  - "${ASDF_REGISTRY_TOOLSET}/toolset:alpine-${ALPINE_VERSION}-${ASDF_VERSION}"   # OS + ASDF version
  - "${ASDF_REGISTRY_TOOLSET}/toolset:alpine-${ALPINE_MAJOR_VERSION}.${ALPINE_MINOR_VERSION}" # OS major.minor
  - "${ASDF_REGISTRY_TOOLSET}/toolset:alpine-${ALPINE_VERSION}-${ASDF_VERSION}-build${BUILD_NUMBER}" # Full with build #

Tag components:

  • alpine / debian — Base operating system
  • ALPINE_VERSION / DEBIAN_VERSION — Full OS version (e.g., 3.20.0, 12.7)
  • ASDF_VERSION — asdf version (e.g., v0.13.0)
  • BUILD_NUMBER — Build iteration counter

Tagging recommendations:

  • Use semantic versioning (v1.0.0) for toolset releases
  • Include the base OS name and version for clarity
  • Add asdf and build metadata for full traceability
  • Set one tag as latest to point to the most recent stable release
  • Use build numbers to distinguish rebuilds of the same version combination

docs.title: Adding Your Own .tool-versions File on Build
docs.text:
You can add your own .tool-versions file, for example:

package_name1 version_number
package_name2 version_number

Naming the file .tool-versions will automatically detect the file and COPY it to the image on build.

To add a custom file (e.g., using a different name), you need to use the Docker COPY command and set ASDF's environment variable correctly:

WORKDIR /tmp/  # Only required if you want to put the file in a different location from the default `/asdf/`
ENV ASDF_TOOL_VERSIONS_FILENAME=custom-tool-version
COPY custom-tool-version ./
RUN bash /init/install-asdf-package

See the asdf reference for more details.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
To get started, follow these steps:

  1. Clone the Repository: Clone this repository to your local environment.
  2. Explore Base Images: Navigate to the base-images directory to explore the base images available.
  3. Customize Dockerfiles: Review and customize the Dockerfiles provided in the base-images directory to suit your needs. Modify the ONBUILD directives, add or remove packages, and adjust versions as required.
  4. Build Specialized Images: Utilize the base images as templates to construct specialized images for your toolsets or software stacks. Refer to the provided examples and adjust the docker-compose files and build configurations according to your preferences.
  5. Run and Test: Once built, run the images in your environment and test to ensure proper functioning.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Contributions to enhance or expand the existing base images or examples are welcome. Feel free to submit pull requests, suggest improvements, or report issues in the repository.

docs.title: Pre-requisites
docs.text: • docker-compose

docs.title: Build Base Images
docs.text: make build

docs.title: Build ToolSet Images
docs.text: make build-toolsets

docs.text:

  • Look at adding a way to sign packages that support it
Could not set tfc-agent to version 1.14.0. Installing ...
Downloading tfc-agent version 1.14.0 from https://releases.hashicorp.com/tfc-agent/1.14.0/tfc-agent_1.14.0_linux_amd64.zip

docs.title: Toolset Docker Images
docs.text: This directory contains example toolset templates that demonstrate how to build specialized Docker images using the base asdf-vm images. Each example is a complete, working toolset that you can use as-is or customize for your needs.

docs.title: What's in This Directory?
docs.text:
toolset-docker-images/
├── helm/              # Example: Kubernetes package manager + related tools
├── awscli/            # Example: AWS CLI and AWS utilities
├── terraform/         # Example: Infrastructure-as-Code tools
└── README.md          # This file

Each toolset directory contains: - Dockerfile - Inherits from a base asdf image and specifies packages to install - docker-compose.yaml - Build configuration with tags, platforms, and versions - .env - Environment variables for versions, registries, and image metadata - README.md - Toolset-specific documentation

docs.title: Quick Start: Using an Example Toolset
Build Locally
docs.text:
Navigate to a toolset directory and build:

cd toolset-docker-images/helm
docker compose build alpine

docs.title: Test the Toolset
docs.text:
docker compose run alpine asdf current
docker compose run alpine helm version

docs.title: Build for a Specific Base
docs.text:
cd toolset-docker-images/terraform
docker compose build debian  # Build on Debian instead of Alpine

docs.title: The Toolset Structure
docs.text: Each toolset is a directory with four key files:

docs.title: 1. Dockerfile
docs.text:
Minimal - just inherits from a base image:

# build with: --build-arg packages="helm helm:version helm:version:set"
ARG asdf_image="ghcr.io/jrosco/asdf-vm:alpine"
FROM $asdf_image

The base image's ONBUILD hooks handle the rest automatically.

docs.title: 2. docker-compose.yaml
docs.text:
Defines how to build the toolset with tags, platforms, and build arguments:

services:
  alpine:
    image: ghcr.io/your-org/helm-toolset:alpine
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
      args:
        asdf_image: "ghcr.io/your-org/asdf-vm:alpine"
        packages: "helm:3.14.0:set helmfile:latest kustomize:latest"
      platforms:
        - "linux/amd64"
      tags:
        - "ghcr.io/your-org/helm-toolset:latest"
        - "ghcr.io/your-org/helm-toolset:alpine-3.20"

docs.title: 3. .env
docs.text:
Configures versions and registries for the build:

# Build metadata
BUILD_NUMBER="0"
ASDF_VERSION="v0.13.0"

# Base image registry
ASDF_REGISTRY_BASE="ghcr.io/your-org/asdf-vm"
ASDF_REGISTRY_TOOLSET="$ASDF_REGISTRY_BASE-toolsets"

# Platform versions
ALPINE_VERSION="3.20"
DEBIAN_VERSION="12.7"

Update these variables in .env before building.

docs.title: 4. README.md
docs.text: Documents the toolset - what tools it includes and how to use it.

docs.title: Step 1: Copy an Existing Example
docs.text:
cp -r toolset-docker-images/helm/ my-custom-toolset/
cd my-custom-toolset/

docs.title: Step 2: Update .env
docs.text:
Customize for your organization and tools:

# Change registry
ASDF_REGISTRY_TOOLSET="ghcr.io/my-org/my-toolsets"

# Define the packages this toolset installs
# (You'll use this in docker-compose.yaml)
MY_TOOL_PACKAGES="terraform:latest:set packer:latest aws-vault:latest"

docs.title: Step 3: Update docker-compose.yaml
docs.text:
Modify the build args to use your packages:

services:
  alpine:
    build:
      args:
        asdf_image: "${ALPINE_IMAGE_REPO_PATH}"
        packages: ${MY_TOOL_PACKAGES}  # Reference from .env

docs.title: Step 4: Update Dockerfile (Optional)
docs.text:
If you need custom setup beyond just installing packages:

ARG asdf_image="ghcr.io/my-org/asdf-vm:alpine"
FROM $asdf_image

# Optional: Add custom scripts or configuration
COPY my-script.sh /usr/local/bin/
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/my-script.sh

docs.title: Step 5: Build and Test
docs.text:
docker compose build alpine
docker compose run alpine asdf current
docker compose run alpine terraform version

docs.title: Understanding Package Arguments
docs.text:
The packages build argument accepts a space-separated list in this format:

package_name:version:modifier

docs.title: Modifiers:
docs.text: Format Example Behavior (no modifier) helm Add plugin only, don't install version helm:3.14.0 Install specific version :latest helm:latest Install latest version :all helm:all Install all versions (rarely used) :set helm:3.14.0:set Install AND set as default

docs.title: Example Packages String:
docs.text:
packages="terraform:1.8.0:set helm:3.14.0:set aws-vault:latest kubectl:1.28.0"

This installs: - Terraform 1.8.0 as default - Helm 3.14.0 as default - Latest AWS Vault - Kubectl 1.28.0

docs.title: Multi-Platform Builds
docs.text:
Build for multiple architectures in docker-compose.yaml:

services:
  alpine:
    build:
      platforms:
        - "linux/amd64"
        - "linux/arm64"
        - "linux/arm/v7"

Then build with buildx:

docker buildx build --push --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 .

docs.title: Build and Tag
docs.text:
docker compose build alpine
docker tag ghcr.io/my-org/my-toolset:latest ghcr.io/my-org/my-toolset:v1.0.0

docs.title: Push to Registry
docs.text:
docker push ghcr.io/my-org/my-toolset:latest
docker push ghcr.io/my-org/my-toolset:v1.0.0

docs.title: Share with Your Team
docs.text:
Users can now pull your toolset:

docker pull ghcr.io/my-org/my-toolset:latest
docker run -it ghcr.io/my-org/my-toolset:latest bash

docs.title: Environment Variables Reference
docs.text:
Common variables used in .env and docker-compose.yaml:

Variable Purpose Example BUILD_NUMBER Build iteration counter 0, 1, 2 ASDF_VERSION asdf version to use v0.13.0 ASDF_REGISTRY_TOOLSET Docker registry for your toolsets ghcr.io/my-org/toolsets ALPINE_VERSION Alpine base image version 3.20 DEBIAN_VERSION Debian base image version 12.7 {TOOL}_PACKAGES Space-separated packages for this toolset helm:latest kubectl:latest

docs.title: Tips & Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Keep Toolsets Focused - Each toolset should solve one problem (Kubernetes tools, AWS tools, Python dev tools, etc.)
  2. Pin Versions - Use specific versions (:3.14.0) for production; use :latest for development
  3. Use .tool-versions for Locked Builds - For critical deployments, use a .tool-versions file
  4. Test Before Publishing - Always test with docker-compose run before pushing to registry
  5. Document Tools - Update each toolset's README with what's included and example usage
  6. Version Your Toolsets - Use semantic versioning (v1.0.0) for your toolset releases

docs.title: Helm Toolset (helm/)
docs.text:
• Base Image: Alpine
  • Tools: Helm, Helmfile, Kustomize
  • Use Case: Kubernetes deployment and package management

docs.title: AWS Toolset (awscli/)
docs.text:
• Base Image: Alpine
  • Tools: AWS CLI, AWS Vault
  • Use Case: AWS infrastructure and secrets management

docs.title: Terraform Toolset (terraform/)
docs.text:
• Base Image: Alpine
  • Tools: Terraform, Packer
  • Use Case: Infrastructure-as-Code provisioning

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
1. Choose an example that's closest to your needs
  2. Copy it to create your own toolset
  3. Update .env with your registry and tool versions
  4. Test locally with docker-compose build and docker-compose run
  5. Customize the Dockerfile if you need special setup
  6. Publish to your registry
  7. Share with your team

See the main README for more information about base images and the asdf version manager.

docs.title: AWS CLI Toolset
docs.text: A Docker image with AWS CLI and related AWS utilities pre-installed for infrastructure and secrets management.

docs.title: Included Tools
docs.text:
• AWS CLI - Official AWS command-line interface
  • AWS Vault - Securely store and access AWS credentials

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
Build the toolset:

docker compose build alpine

Test it:

docker compose run alpine aws --version
docker compose run alpine aws-vault --version
docker compose run alpine asdf current

docs.title: Customization
docs.text:
Edit .env to change versions or registry:

AWSCLI_PACKAGES="awscli:2.13.0:set aws-vault:latest:set"
ASDF_REGISTRY_TOOLSET="ghcr.io/your-org/toolsets"

Then rebuild:

docker compose build alpine

docs.title: More Info
docs.text: See Toolsets Overview for detailed build and customization instructions.

docs.title: Helm Toolset
docs.text: A Docker image with Helm, Helmfile, and Helm Diff pre-installed for Kubernetes package management and deployment.

docs.title: Included Tools
docs.text:
• Helm - Kubernetes package manager and templating engine
  • Helmfile - Declarative Helm charts management
  • Helm Diff - Preview Helm upgrades before applying

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
Build the toolset:

docker compose build alpine

Test it:

docker compose run alpine helm version
docker compose run alpine helmfile --version
docker compose run alpine asdf current

docs.title: Customization
docs.text:
Edit .env to change versions or registry:

HELM_PACKAGES="helm:3.14.0:set helmfile:latest:set helm-diff:latest:set"
ASDF_REGISTRY_TOOLSET="ghcr.io/your-org/toolsets"

Then rebuild:

docker compose build alpine

docs.title: More Info
docs.text: See Toolsets Overview for detailed build and customization instructions.

docs.title: Terraform Toolset
docs.text: A Docker image with Terraform, TFLint, and Terraform Validator pre-installed for infrastructure-as-code provisioning and validation.

docs.title: Included Tools
docs.text:
• Terraform - Infrastructure-as-code provisioning engine
  • TFLint - Linter and best-practice validator for Terraform
  • Terraform Validator - Policy validation for Terraform configurations

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
Build the toolset:

docker compose build alpine

Test it:

docker compose run alpine terraform version
docker compose run alpine tflint --version
docker compose run alpine asdf current

docs.title: Customization
docs.text:
Edit .env to change versions or registry:

TERRAFORM_PACKAGES="terraform:1.8.0:set tflint:0.50.0:set terraform-validator:latest:set"
ASDF_REGISTRY_TOOLSET="ghcr.io/your-org/toolsets"

Then rebuild:

docker compose build alpine

docs.title: More Info
docs.text: See Toolsets Overview for detailed build and customization instructions.
